reasoning: The bullish consensus rests on a weak technical foundation masked by unanimous directional alignment. The ribbon spread of 27¢ is BELOW the 30¢ conviction threshold that Macro itself flagged; this is not strong bull structure, it is aligned-but-compressed. More critically, the move is powered EXCLUSIVELY by XLF (+0.77%), while XLK (largest sector) is dead at +0.22% and XLE is DOWN -1.49%. This is single-sector financial leadership on a rate-sensitive trade, not broad risk-on. The Internals agent rated this at only 0.60 confidence with explicit warning: 'vulnerable to reversal if energy stabilizes or tech rolls over.' The battle level at 681.16 (2-star Carry, only $1.03 above current) sits in a tightly-packed overhead resistance cluster that premarket already tested at 680.50. If energy continues to underperform, tech fails to participate, or VIX reverses direction, this bullish thesis collapses within the first hour of RTH.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: VIX spikes above 20.00 in first 30 minutes of RTH (from 18.99 base)
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Volatility re-expansion destroys the macro bull thesis (VIX compression + falling regime). Bullish momentum historically stalls on VIX reversals; this would invalidate the low-vol directional trade setup entirely.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: SPY opens below 680.00 AND XLE continues to weaken (break below -1.49% premarket loss)
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Single-sector XLF leadership fails if energy rolls over further. The rate-sensitive (banks up, energy down) narrative reveals itself as incomplete; without energy stabilization, the macro backdrop becomes genuinely risk-off, not risk-on.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: SPY rejects premarket high at 680.50 within first hour of RTH and closes below 679.80
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Setup proves to be range-bound chop, not breakout momentum. Price oscillates between slow EMA 679.45 and premarket ceiling 680.50, confirming the ribbon compression (27¢) is too weak to sustain directional conviction. Bull case collapses to sideways consolidation.

weakest_consensus_link: Single-sector (XLF-only) narrow-breadth leadership combined with sub-conviction ribbon spread (27¢ < 30¢). Without broad sector participation or higher ribbon compression, the bull move is vulnerable to early mean reversion and sector rotation reversal.
key_observations: Ribbon spread 27¢ is BELOW the 30¢ conviction floor that Macro itself identified — this is aligned but not conviction-level compression
XLF drives +0.77% while XLK is dead at +0.22% and XLE DOWN -1.49% — this is single-sector leadership, not broad bullish participation
Internals agent explicitly warns 'vulnerable to reversal if energy stabilizes or tech rolls over' at only 0.60 confidence (lowest in the group) — the consensus has not addressed this fragility
Premarket already tested 680.50 (PMH); the battle level at 681.16 is only $0.66 above that — tightly-packed overhead resistance could cap the move or reject it early

Sector divergence (rate-positive/energy-weak) reveals a narrow macro narrative: financials benefiting from rate environment, but commodity/energy markets not confirming broad risk-on. This is fragile to sector rotation.
Consensus agents have not adequately weighted the scenario where XLE stabilizes (mean-reversion bounce) or XLK awakens — if either occurs, the XLF-driven move loses power immediately
